skill.name: reactbits
skill.description: Search, browse, and install animated React components from ReactBits.dev (135+ components). Use when the user wants to add UI effects, animations, backgrounds, or interactive components to their React/Next.js project.
skill.summary: Find and integrate animated ReactBits components for React and Next.js projects.
skill.author: hwj23016021425

security_audit.summary: Static shell-execution and weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown code spans and ordinary descriptive text. The skill still carries medium risk because it instructs agents to install dependencies and write component files during project integration.

medium_findings.title: Dependency Installation and File Modification Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to install missing packages, write component files, and create CSS files. This is legitimate for a React component skill, but it can change project dependencies and source files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly describe dependency installation and file creation. The workflow appears legitimate, but it has direct project modification impact.

low_findings.title: False Positive Markdown Tool Names Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static shell-execution alerts point to Markdown inline code spans that name MCP tools and usage steps. No shell command, Ruby backtick execution, or command interpolation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain documentation syntax for tool names, arguments, and file paths. They are not executable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts occur in descriptive text and component guidance. No cryptographic algorithm, key handling, hashing API, or security-sensitive encryption workflow appears on the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found normal prose and package names only. There is no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA-1, or other weak cryptographic use.

content.value_statement: Teams often need polished React motion components without building every effect from scratch. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code find ReactBits components and integrate them into React or Next.js projects.
actual_capabilities: Lists ReactBits component categories through MCP tools.
Searches components by query, category, style, and limit.
Retrieves full component source for selected ReactBits components.
Retrieves usage demos for selected components.
Guides integration into React and Next.js project files.
Prompts website builders for purpose, style, color, pages, and features.
limitations: Requires access to the ReactBits MCP server.
Does not include component source directly in the skill file.
May require package installation for animation libraries.
Generated integrations still need project-specific testing.

use_cases.title: Enhance a Landing Page
use_cases.description: Find animated backgrounds, text effects, and interactive sections that fit a product page.

use_cases.title: Prototype Visual Effects
use_cases.description: Compare ReactBits components quickly before choosing one for a prototype or demo.

use_cases.title: Build a Portfolio Site
use_cases.description: Select polished motion components for project galleries, navigation, and hero sections.

prompt_templates.title: Browse Categories
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the ReactBits categories and recommend a few options for a modern React page.

prompt_templates.title: Search by Effect
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ReactBits for animated text components that work well in a Next.js hero section.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Component Choices
prompt_templates.prompt: Find three ReactBits background components for a SaaS dashboard and compare their visual fit.

prompt_templates.title: Integrate Selected Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the Tailwind TypeScript version of the chosen ReactBits component and integrate it into my page.

output_examples.input: I need an animated hero section for a Next.js app.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists relevant ReactBits categories, recommends matching components, and asks which effect style to install.

output_examples.input: Add a polished loading animation to my React project.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches loader components, retrieves the selected source and demo, then explains required dependencies.

output_examples.input: Help me choose components for a portfolio site.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks about style, color, pages, and key features before recommending ReactBits components.

best_practices: Review retrieved component code before adding it to production projects.
Confirm package installations before changing project dependencies.
Test animation performance on mobile and low-power devices.
anti_patterns: Do not install dependencies without user approval.
Do not add multiple heavy animation components without testing performance.
Do not assume every ReactBits component fits the existing design system.

faq.question: What does this skill connect to?
faq.answer: It uses the ReactBits MCP server to list, search, retrieve, and demo ReactBits components.

faq.question: Does it support Next.js?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is intended for React and Next.js projects.

faq.question: Can it install dependencies?
faq.answer: It can guide dependency installation, but users should approve package changes first.

faq.question: Does the skill contain component code?
faq.answer: No. It retrieves component source through the configured MCP tools.

faq.question: Which styles are supported?
faq.answer: The skill references Tailwind, CSS, default, and TypeScript component variants.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a marketplace warning for dependency installation and file changes.
